elements.sent-2411-003: This paper studies the pool strategy for price-makers under imperfect information.
elements.sent-2411-004: In this occasion, market participants cannot obtain essential transmission parameters of the power system.
elements.sent-2411-005: Thus, price-makers should estimate the market results with respect to their offer curves using available historical information.
elements.sent-2411-006: The linear programming model of economic dispatch is analyzed with the theory of rim multi-parametric linear programming (rim-MPLP).
elements.sent-2411-007: The characteristics of system patterns (combinations of status flags for generating units and transmission lines) are revealed.
elements.sent-2411-008: A multi-class classification model based on support vector machine (SVM) is trained to map the offer curves to system patterns, which is then integrated into the decision framework of the price-maker.
elements.sent-2411-009: The performance of the proposed method is validated on the IEEE 30-bus system, Illinois synthetic 200-bus system, and South Carolina synthetic 500-bus system.

elements.sent-2411-011: In electricity markets, they usually choose to bid strategically and their behaviors are studied in optimization-based frameworks ever since the beginning of market deregulation~\cite{david2000strategic,LI20114686 .}
elements.sent-2411-012: In their strategies, both clearing prices and capacity should be considered as endogenous variables formed by the offer curves~\cite{tao2005strategic , and their estimation of the potential market structure as well as the supply and demand situation underlies such endogenous formation.}
elements.sent-2411-013: The modeling of price-makers' pool strategies is beneficial for both market participants and market organizers, as it helps in market simulation, profitability estimation, and market power evaluation.
elements.sent-2411-014: Currently, the spot electricity market is usually cleared using economic dispatch (ED) and locational marginal price (LMP) scheme~\cite{Litvinov2010lmp .}
elements.sent-2411-015: The system operator collects the offers and the bids from market participants, constructs the ED optimization problem with some constraints and a welfare maximization objective, and then solves the problem ahead of the trading time.
elements.sent-2411-016: The clearing prices are calculated with the dual variables of the energy balance and security constraints (a.k.a.
elements.sent-2411-017: To ensure solvability and optimality, the objective function is assumed to be convex (linear or quadratic), and the constraints are assumed to be linear.
elements.sent-2411-018: Since essential transmission parameters are considered to be static during a certain time period, the inputs of the ED problem are mainly load forecasts and market bids and offers.
elements.sent-2411-019: Market information is very important for the decision of pool strategies for price-makers.
elements.sent-2411-020: Under perfect information, market participants have access to all market data and transmission parameters.
elements.sent-2411-021: Thus, they can construct the precise mathematical model of the ED problem and integrate it into their decision model to form a bilevel optimization problem~\cite{Hobbs2000strategic .}
elements.sent-2411-022: However, when the information is imperfect, the market participants may not have access to transmission parameters and some market data.
elements.sent-2411-023: Their decision model should rely on the estimated or inferred market model from available market data.
elements.sent-2411-024: Existing research in the field of price-makers' pool strategies falls into two main categories: residual demand curve (RDC)-based methods~\cite{conejo2002optimal,song2018price,xu2011bidding,portela2017residual and mathematical program with equilibrium constraints (MPEC)-based methods~\cite{Hobbs2000strategic,ruiz2009pool,zugno2013pool,ruiz2014tutorial,ghamkhari2017strategic .. The RDC (a.k.a.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: In its original form, RDC only takes the market supply and demand situation into consideration and ignores the impact resulting from transmission congestion.
elements.sent-2411-026: In~\cite{conejo2002optimal , RDC was integrated into the constraints of GenCo' decision model.}
elements.sent-2411-027: Since RDC is stepwise which makes the optimization problem nonlinear, a coordination-descent technique coupled with mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) techniques was proposed.
elements.sent-2411-028: In~\cite{song2018price , the price-maker bidding problem of a retailer with flexible demands was modeled with RDC and solved with MILP techniques.}
elements.sent-2411-029: Xu et al. extended RDC to apply in occasions when transmission constraints are binding and proposed the concept of transmission-constrained residual demand derivative (TCRDD)~\cite{xu2011bidding .}
elements.sent-2411-030: TCRDD is regarded as sensitivity analysis of the optimal power flow (OPF)\footnote{Since DC-OPF is widely used in electricity markets, the abbreviation OPF stands for DC-OPF rather than AC-OPF in this paper.}
elements.sent-2411-031: Gonz\'{a lez et al. extended RDC to consider complex offering conditions in the Iberian electricity market~\cite{portela2017residual .}}
elements.sent-2411-032: The MPEC-based methods nest the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions of OPF in the GenCo's profit maximization problem.
elements.sent-2411-033: By linearizing the product term using the strong duality condition and the complementarity conditions using the big-M method, the MPEC model can be transformed into an MILP problem~\cite{ruiz2009pool .}
elements.sent-2411-034: In~\cite{Hobbs2000strategic , a penalty interior point algorithm was used to compute a local optimal solution of the MPEC.}
elements.sent-2411-035: In~\cite{ruiz2009pool , uncertainties of consumers' bids and rival producers' offers are modeled in a scenario-based approach in MPEC and solved using MILP.}
elements.sent-2411-036: In~\cite{zugno2013pool , MPEC was used to model the pool strategy of a price-maker wind power producer in the balancing market.}
elements.sent-2411-037: In~\cite{ye2016mpec , the impact of energy storage on GenCo's strategic behaviors was modeled with MPEC.}
elements.sent-2411-038: The market power of large GenCos in imperfect electricity market can be limited by the energy storage system.
elements.sent-2411-039: Ruiz et al. provided a tutorial review on the application of MPEC-related complementarity models in energy market decision problems~\cite{ruiz2014tutorial .}
elements.sent-2411-040: In~\cite{ghamkhari2017strategic , the problem of high computational burden in the traditional MILP formulation of MPEC was discussed.}
elements.sent-2411-041: A convex relaxation method based on Schmudgen's Positivstellensatz Theorem in semialgebraic geometry is proposed to obtain efficient solutions.
elements.sent-2411-042: In~\cite{sepetanc2020cluster , the optimal bidding and scheduling of aggregated battery swapping stations was studied using MPEC.}
elements.sent-2411-043: For the RDC-based methods, the main challenge is the modeling of price-makers' influence on the binding constraints of OPF, as RDC and TCRDD are constructed under fixed binding constraints.
elements.sent-2411-044: Although MPEC-based methods have a well-established and solid mathematical foundation and can precisely model price-makers' influence on market results, they are applicable only if perfect market information is available.
elements.sent-2411-045: Several existing articles~\cite{wen2001optimal,li2005strategic,bompard2021market discussed the strategic bidding problem of price-maker generators under imperfect information.}
elements.sent-2411-046: In~\cite{wen2001optimal , the bidding problem was formulated in an RDC-like approach where the supply functions of generators were modeled with linear functions and transmission capacity constraints were ignored.}
elements.sent-2411-047: It was assumed that each generator did not know the supply functions of its rivals.
elements.sent-2411-048: In~\cite{li2005strategic , the GenCo's decision problem was modeled using a bilevel problem, and the lower level problem was OPF.}
elements.sent-2411-049: Two types of cost coefficients were used to model the unknown supply functions of GenCo's rivals.
elements.sent-2411-050: In~\cite{bompard2021market , the market equilibrium for a sequential game of a bilateral energy market was analyzed assuming each player had no access to all action of other players that moved before him at every state of the game.}
elements.sent-2411-051: It is shown that existing discussion on bidding strategy under imperfect information mainly focuses on the modeling of rivals' unknown supply functions, while bidding strategy in absence of transmission topology and parameters is still a problem.
elements.sent-2411-052: Thus, this paper tries to propose a data-driven pool strategy for price-makers under imperfect information, which makes it more applicable in real markets.
elements.sent-2411-053: The OPF problem is analyzed and modeled using multi-parametric linear programming (MPLP) theory (rim-MPLP, more specifically).
elements.sent-2411-054: The impact of GenCo' bids on the binding constraints is learned using a multi-class support vector machine (SVM) classifier.
elements.sent-2411-055: The classifier is then integrated into the GenCo's decision framework which can be solved using gradient descent methods.
elements.sent-2411-056: The proposed method solves the decision problem in an approach similar to the MPEC-based methods.
elements.sent-2411-057: The major difference is that the proposed method uses a data-driven model instead of the precise OPF in MPEC to model the market outcomes.
elements.sent-2411-058: The idea of using MPLP theory in the bilevel market decision problem is inspired by reference~\cite{faisca2007parametric,bylling2020parametric .}
elements.sent-2411-059: In~\cite{faisca2007parametric , it is shown that various classes of bilevel problems can be transformed into a set of independent single level problems using MPLP theory.}
elements.sent-2411-060: Such transformation helps in obtaining global optimality.
elements.sent-2411-061: In~\cite{bylling2020parametric , linearly constrained bilevel programming problems with lower-level primal and dual optimal solutions in the upper-level objective function (BPP-Ds) are specifically considered.}
elements.sent-2411-062: When the parametric function of the lower level problem can be obtained, BPP-Ds can be solved efficiently.
elements.sent-2411-063: Cases of generation and transmission investment are also performed.
elements.sent-2411-064: However, obtaining the parametric functions of the lower level problem also requires a precise OPF model, which limits the direct application of MPLP under imperfect information.
elements.sent-2411-065: An important concept in MPLP theory is the critical region (CR) which is also referred to as the system pattern or system pattern region (SPR) when analyzing the OPF problem.
elements.sent-2411-066: Each CR or system pattern corresponds to a unique set of binding constraints in OPF, i.e., combination of status flags for generating units and transmission lines.
elements.sent-2411-067: The shadow prices and optimal solutions of OPF are linear-affine functions within each CR.
elements.sent-2411-068: More theoretical analysis on CR will be provided in Section~\ref{sec:mplp .}
elements.sent-2411-070: Compared with the aforementioned research, this paper makes the following contributions:.
elements.sent-2411-071: Electricity markets' basic OPF models are analyzed using rim-MPLP with generation bids and nodal load as parameters of the linear programming problem.
elements.sent-2411-072: The characteristics of critical regions and parametric functions within each CR are revealed.
elements.sent-2411-073: An integratable model based on machine learning (specifically a multi-class classifier) is proposed to learn and utilize the probabilistic map between the price-maker's bid and system patterns from available market data.
elements.sent-2411-074: The model can be solved using gradient descent methods and can help the price-maker decide its optimal bidding curve under imperfect information.
elements.sent-2411-075: Numerical experiments based on the IEEE 30-bus system, Illinois synthetic 200-bus system, and South Carolina synthetic 500-bus system are conducted to demonstrate the performance of the proposed method.

elements.sent-2411-077: Section~\ref{sec:mplp presents theoretical analysis on OPF with rim MPLP.}
elements.sent-2411-078: Section~\ref{sec:learning details the learning of system patterns using multi-class classification models.}
elements.sent-2411-079: Section~\ref{sec:methodology gives the formulation of the price-maker's optimization problem and its solution method.}
elements.sent-2411-080: The case study is conducted in Section~\ref{sec:case .}

elements.sent-2411-192: To illustrate the application of the data-driven pool strategy, a case study based on simulated market data is conducted.
elements.sent-2411-193: We perform the case study using the IEEE 30-bus system, Illinois synthetic 200-bus system, and South Carolina synthetic 500-bus system~\cite{birchfield2017grid whose data are provided in Matpower~\cite{matpower .}}
elements.sent-2411-194: In the 30-bus system, all 6 generators are assumed to be strategic.
elements.sent-2411-195: In the 200-bus system, generators \#26, \#27, \#28, \#29, \#30, and \#37 are assumed to be strategic, while the other 43 generators that have small capacity or low generation cost are assumed to be nonstrategic.
elements.sent-2411-196: In the 500-bus system, 14 generators with generation capacity of more than \SI{100 {MW and relatively higher generation cost are set as strategic, while other 42 generators are set as nonstrategic.}}
elements.sent-2411-197: It is assumed that generators can price away freely from their true cost.
elements.sent-2411-198: All numerical experiments are conducted on an Intel Core i9-10900K@3.70 GHz desktop with MATLAB 2020b.
elements.sent-2411-200: For each test system, both the block form and the quadratic form of bid curves as well as both lossless and lossy OPF models are considered.
elements.sent-2411-201: For the block form, the number of price blocks $ B $ is set as 5, including 2 nonstrategic blocks (which are set at a sufficiently low price to ensure a minimum income) for the 30-bus system case and 1 nonstrategic block for the 200-bus system and 500-bus system.
elements.sent-2411-202: Each block has the same volume in the basic scenario.
elements.sent-2411-203: For each case (combination of different systems and block/quadratic and lossless/lossy), 8\,760 scenarios are generated by adding 10\% Gaussian deviation on $ \boldsymbol{c _{i,b $ and $ \boldsymbol{Q _{i,b ^{U $ or $ \boldsymbol{b _{i $ of the basic scenario.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-204: The nodal load scenarios are generated using the one-year hourly load scenarios of the Illinois synthetic 200-bus system contained in the Matpower scenario file.
elements.sent-2411-205: The whole dataset is split into two sets: the training \& validation set (80\%) and the test set (20\%).
elements.sent-2411-206: The training and validation procedure is performed in a 5-fold cross validation approach.
elements.sent-2411-207: The best hyperparameter $ C $ in SVM is chosen from $ \{0.1,1,10,10,100,10^3, 10^4\}$.

elements.subsection-2411-012: Discussion on Scalability to Larger Systems
elements.sent-2411-265: Generally, as the power system become larger to thousands of nodes, the electricity market would become more competitive.
elements.sent-2411-266: It is difficult for GenCos to seek extra revenue in a perfect competitive market.
elements.sent-2411-267: From the case study, it is shown that the proposed method could extract useful information from market data and support bidding decision in a moderate-sized power system.
elements.sent-2411-268: If the CR is not complex in a large system, the performance of the proposed method is expected to be similar to the performance in moderated-sized cases.
elements.sent-2411-269: If the CR is too complex, other strategies (e.g., RDC-based methods) are preferred.
elements.sent-2411-270: This paper proposes a data-driven pool strategy for price-makers under imperfect information.
elements.sent-2411-271: Based on general OPF models, the critical region and system pattern characteristics of the market clearing model are analyzed using rim-MPLP theory.
elements.sent-2411-272: Then, such characteristics are used for the training of multi-class classifier with historical market data.
elements.sent-2411-273: The parametric functions of optimal dual and primal variables in OPF are fitted with linear regression.
elements.sent-2411-274: The learned classifier and parametric functions are integrated into the price-makers decision framework to form a revenue maximization problem.
elements.sent-2411-275: We then show that such problem can be solved using gradient descent-based methods.
elements.sent-2411-276: The proposed method can consider the impact of GenCo's bidding on both the market binding constraints and market clearing prices.
elements.sent-2411-277: It can be regarded as an extension of the famous MPEC method under imperfect information when essential power system parameters are unavailable.
elements.sent-2411-278: The proposed method can be used for both market participants and market organizers in decision-making, behavior evaluation and simulation.
elements.sent-2411-279: A case study based on the IEEE 30-bus system, Illinois 200-bus system, and South Carolina 500-bus system demonstrates the performance of the proposed method in small and moderate-sized systems.
elements.sent-2411-280: Future work includes considering transmission network contingency in the method.. \appendices.
